steps.title: Translate the intercept
steps.content: An $x$-intercept is a point where the graph has $y=0$. Since $(-7,0)$ is an $x$-intercept, the function value at $x=-7$ is $0$.

steps.title: Match function notation
steps.content: The statement $f(-7)=0$ says exactly that the point $(-7,0)$ is on the graph. The answer is C.
